title_en: Robert Rauschenberg’s Penchant for Invention and Spectacle
title_zh: 罗伯特·劳申伯格对发明与奇观的执着
rubric_en: In 2007, he and Merce Cunningham put a new twist on a famous 1981 sculpture.
rubric_zh: 2007 年，他与默斯·坎宁安在一件著名的 1981 年雕塑作品上做出了全新的演绎。

paragraphs.en: Robert Rauschenberg and Merce Cunningham with Rauschenberg’s 1981 sculpture “The Ancient Incident (Kabal American Zephyr)” at his Captiva Island studio in Florida, 2007 (Laurence Getford / Robert Rauschenberg Foundation Archives)
paragraphs.zh: 2007 年，在佛罗里达卡普蒂瓦岛工作室，罗伯特·劳申伯格与默斯·坎宁安与劳申伯格于 1981 年创作的雕塑《古老事件（Kabal American Zephyr）》合影留念（劳伦斯·盖特福德 / 罗伯特·劳申伯格基金会档案）

paragraphs.en: Collaboration, for Robert Rauschenberg and Merce Cunningham, began with the arrangement of chairs.
paragraphs.zh: 对罗伯特·劳申伯格和默斯·坎宁安来说，合作始于椅子的摆放。

paragraphs.en: In the summer of 1952, when Rauschenberg was 26 and Cunningham was 33, both were living at Black Mountain College, a nontraditional art school in North Carolina that encouraged exchange and experimentation among its students and faculty. One August night, the composer John Cage—Cunningham’s partner—convened an impromptu performance in the dining hall featuring Rauschenberg, Cunningham, and others. No one took any pictures of the event, and firsthand accounts and recollections vary. Cunningham, who later became famous as a choreographer, was chased by a dog as he danced. Rauschenberg, a pathbreaking visual artist, seems to have provided paintings for the “set,” and played old songs on a gramophone. There was no script, and certainly no rehearsal—but not everything was left to chance. Chairs were carefully laid out in a square, in the middle of the action, so that audience members could look at one another while they watched the performers. The people were part of the art, and not knowing exactly what, or whom, to focus on at any given moment was the point.
paragraphs.zh: 1952 年夏天，时年 26 岁的劳申伯格与 33 岁的坎宁安都生活在北卡罗来纳州的黑山学院，这是一所鼓励学生与教师之间交流与实验的非传统艺术学校。有一个八月之夜，作曲家约翰·凯奇——坎宁安的伴侣——在餐厅里即兴组织了一场汇演，参与者包括劳申伯格、坎宁安和其他人。无人对这一事件拍照，且当时的第一手记述和回忆各不相同。后来成为著名编舞家的坎宁安在舞蹈时被一条狗追赶。开创性的视觉艺术家劳申伯格似乎为所谓的“舞台”提供了画作，并在留声机上放了些老歌。现场没有剧本，也当然没有排练，但并非一切都交由偶然决定。椅子被小心地摆成一个方阵，正处在表演核心，使观众在观看表演者时也能互相看见彼此。观众本身成为了作品的一部分，而在任何时刻都不知道该盯着什么、盯着谁，正是这次表演的重点。

paragraphs.en: Cunningham and Rauschenberg remained friends and collaborators. Of his work with Cunningham, Rauschenberg reportedly said that he felt more at home with dancers than he did in the world of painting: “The idea of having your own body and its activity be the material—that was really tempting.” Much of Rauschenberg’s art was three-dimensional; he was widely celebrated for his “combines,” which incorporated found objects into canvases and freestanding sculptures. In a 1981 sculpture, The Ancient Incident (Kabal American Zephyr), he used wood-and-metal stands and two wooden chairs to create a pyramid of sorts—an elegant, if impractical, seating arrangement.
paragraphs.zh: 坎宁安与劳申伯格始终保持友谊与合作。劳申伯格据说谈及与坎宁安的合作时说过，他在舞者的世界里比在绘画领域里更有归属感：“让你的身体及其行动本身成为材料——这是真的很有吸引力。”劳申伯格的大量作品是三维的；他以“组合体”闻名，将现成物件并入画布与独立雕塑之中。在一件 1981 年的雕塑《古老事件（Kabal American Zephyr）》中，他用了木金属支架和两把木椅，拼出一种类似金字塔的结构——一种优雅却不实用的座位布局。

paragraphs.en: By 2007, Rauschenberg and Cunningham were 81 and 87, respectively. They still had a penchant for invention and spectacle—and play. At Rauschenberg’s home and studio on Captiva Island, in Florida, after Rauschenberg challenged Cunningham to a wheelchair race, they posed in front of The Ancient Incident, adding another layer of chairs, as well as their own bodies, to the 26-year-old sculpture. As Rauschenberg’s wry grin makes clear, the old friends thoroughly enjoyed their fresh act of mutual creation.
paragraphs.zh: 到2007年，劳申伯格和坎宁汉姆分别已经81岁和87岁。他们仍然热衷于创新、奇观，以及游戏般的创作。2007年，在佛罗里达州卡蒂瓦岛上劳申伯格的家和工作室里，在劳申伯格向坎宁汉姆发起一场轮椅赛之后，他们一起在《古老事件》前摆起了姿势，为这件有26年历史的雕塑又叠加了一层椅子，以及他们的身体。劳申伯格那带点讥讽意味的笑容表明，这对老朋友非常享受这场彼此共同创作的全新表演。

paragraphs.en: This article appears in the May 2026 print edition with the headline “New Chairs.”
paragraphs.zh: 这篇文章刊登在2026年5月的纸媒版块头条，标题为“New Chairs”（新椅子）。
